metadata.title: 5th Pass Debug Factorial — GCP SkyPilot Launch + Cloud Test Suite Hardening
metadata.priority: P0 — first clean launch after comprehensive QA (49 new tests, ALL preflight passed)
metadata.estimated-cost: ~$5-10 GCP L4 spot (32 GPU jobs + 2 zero-shot, 2 epochs each)

root-causes.cause: job_recovery field removed in SkyPilot v1.0 (YAML parse failure)
sky binary not found (PATH not including .venv/bin)
Wrong splits file hardcoded in setup script
No monitoring — 8 failures undetected for 2+ hours

fixes-applied.fix: Fixed run_factorial.sh to find sky in .venv
Fixed setup script splits file selection (debug vs production)
Factorial monitor skill upgrade plan written

qa-improvements-since-4th-pass.improvement: SkyPilot YAML tests: 22 → 56 (file_mounts, disk_size, DVC init, escape hatches, ALL 10 YAMLs)
Cross-flow FlowContract tests: 11 new (log/find roundtrip — critical gap closed)
Prod gate: 34 skips → 1 (cloud tests excluded, only VRAM-gated SAM3 TopoLoRA)
Integration test decision matrix: 4 reviewer agents, 28 hypotheses analyzed
DeepVess data: 1.45 GB downloaded, DVC tracked, 27 files on GCS
Factorial dry-run: 34 conditions validated (32 trainable + 2 zero-shot)

mandate.name: Platform Validation (Primary)
mandate.description: Validate that the FULL factorial pipeline works end-to-end on GCP: Docker pull → DVC data → model training → SWAG → metrics → MLflow. This is a PLATFORM paper (Nature Protocols), not SOTA. The contribution is the pipeline capability, not the segmentation numbers.

success-criteria.criterion: All 34 conditions reach SUCCEEDED state (32 trainable + 2 zero-shot)
MLflow has finite val/dice for every condition
SWAG uncertainty estimates are finite (not NaN)
Checkpoint files exist on GCS for every fold
Docker image pull completes in <15 min
DVC data pull from GCS succeeds on every VM

mandate.name: Cloud Test Suite Hardening (Secondary)
mandate.description: Use observations from the GCP run to identify edge cases and improve the cloud test suite (tests/v2/cloud/). Current state: 33 cloud tests that all skip locally. Goal: make them comprehensive and actually useful when run with credentials.

focus-areas.area: MLflow cloud CRUD — verify experiment creation, metric logging, artifact upload against real server
SkyPilot job lifecycle — test PENDING→RUNNING→SUCCEEDED transitions with real sky jobs queue
DVC remote sync — verify dvc pull from GCS works, checksums match
Docker GAR pull timing — measure and assert pull time is within budget
Spot preemption handling — verify checkpoint persistence across preemption events
Cross-cloud metric visibility — verify MLflow metrics logged from GCP VM are queryable locally
Cost estimation accuracy — compare actual vs estimated cost per job
FAILED_SETUP diagnosis — verify ralph_monitor patterns catch real failures

factorial-design.summary: Total conditions: 4×4×2 × 2×2 × 5 = 640 (IDENTICAL to production) Debug scope reductions (ONLY 3): 1 fold, 2 epochs, half data GPU jobs: 32 cells × 1 fold = 32 (each runs training + SWAG internally) Zero-shot baselines: 2 (SAM3 Vanilla on MiniVess, VesselFM on DeepVess)

phases.phase: Poll sky jobs queue every 60s. Track state transitions: PENDING → INIT → SETTING_UP → RUNNING → SUCCEEDED/FAILED. Kill-switch: if 3+ jobs fail with IDENTICAL error within 5 min, cancel same-config jobs.
On terminal state, aggregate failures by root cause. Use ralph_monitor.py patterns for classification. Create per-root-cause diagnosis with affected conditions.
Batch fix per root cause (never serial whac-a-mole). Use self-learning-iterative-coder for code fixes. Write cloud test for each discovered failure pattern.
Rebuild Docker image if code changed (Rule F3). Relaunch ONLY failed conditions (max 2 cycles, Rule F4).

Write final report to docs/planning/v0-2_archive/original_docs/run-debug-factorial-experiment-report-5th-pass.md Include: success/failure matrix, cost breakdown, MLflow metrics summary, cloud test improvements made, issues created.

cloud-test-improvement-protocol.description: For EVERY failure observed during monitoring, the FIX phase MUST include a new cloud test that would catch the same failure locally or with credentials. This is how we systematically upgrade tests/v2/cloud/ from 33 skip-only tests to a comprehensive cloud validation suite.

categories.category: DVC pull, Docker pull, env var resolution
OOM, NaN, CUDA errors, model loading
MLflow upload, checkpoint persistence, metric logging
Spot preemption, network timeout, quota exhaustion

watchlist.item: FAILED_SETUP within first 5 minutes — indicates YAML/Docker/DVC issues. Previously: 8 jobs failed silently for 2+ hours. New: factorial-monitor polls every 60s with desktop notification.
SAM3 models FP16 overflow → NaN during validation. Fix: BF16 enforced. T4 banned. Only L4/A100 (Ada+) allowed. Verify: val/dice is finite for SAM3 conditions.
Docker pull time from GAR. 9 GB image. Measure: actual pull time per job from sky jobs logs. Target: <10 min (currently ~15 min).
DVC pull from GCS to spot VM. ~2.7 GB MiniVess data. Verify: data-exists check prevents redundant pulls on spot recovery.
SWAG uncertainty estimates. First time running SWAG on cloud. Verify: swag_uncertainty metric is finite (not NaN/Inf).
Spot preemption recovery. May or may not trigger in debug run. If triggered: verify checkpoint persistence via MOUNT_CACHED.
MambaVesselNet++ CUDA compilation. mamba-ssm requires CUDA toolkit. Verify: mamba_ssm imports successfully on cloud VM.
Cross-flow FlowContract. First time the full train→post_training→analysis chain runs on cloud. Verify: find_upstream_run() discovers training runs on cloud MLflow.

cloud-test-upgrade.current-state: 33 tests in tests/v2/cloud/ — all skip without MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI. Files: test_cloud_mlflow.py (16), test_cloud_training_artifacts.py (3), test_hf_accessibility.py (2), test_preflight_validation.py (3), test_skypilot_mlflow.py (3), test_skypilot_runpod.py (3), test_training_flow_cloud_mlflow.py (3).
cloud-test-upgrade.target-state: 60+ tests covering: MLflow CRUD on real server, SkyPilot job lifecycle, DVC remote sync, Docker GAR pull validation, checkpoint persistence, cross-cloud metric visibility, cost estimation accuracy, FAILED_SETUP pattern matching. Parametrized for both RunPod and GCP backends.

test-group.name: SkyPilot Job Lifecycle
test-group.test: test_failed_setup_detected — verify FAILED_SETUP is caught by ralph_monitor patterns
test_job_logs_contain_training_output — verify sky jobs logs has epoch output

test-group.name: MLflow Cloud Metrics
test-group.test: test_cloud_mlflow_experiment_creation — create experiment on remote server
test_cloud_mlflow_metric_logging — log val/dice metric and read back
test_cloud_mlflow_artifact_upload — upload and download small artifact
test_cloud_mlflow_search_runs — search by tags like flow_name

test-group.name: DVC Cloud Sync
test-group.test: test_dvc_gcs_pull_data_exists — verify data accessible on GCS

test-group.name: Docker GAR
test-group.test: test_gar_image_has_required_packages — verify mamba_ssm, monai in image
test_gar_image_freshness — image built after latest src/ commit

test-group.name: Cost Estimation
test-group.test: test_per_job_cost_reasonable — no single job exceeds $1 (debug run)
